seo.title: Building Data Extraction with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building data extraction agents with OpenAI Agents SDK enables schema-guided extraction from unstructured documents using multi-agent handoffs and structured outputs. Guardrails are essential to prevent handoff loops, ensure schema compliance, validate field accuracy, and maintain type consistency in production systems. Vex integrates seamlessly as output guardrails to catch these failures before they cascade.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with OpenAI Agents SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture starts with a Router Agent that classifies documents and hands off to specialized Extractor Agents (e.g., InvoiceExtractor, ContractExtractor). Each extractor uses schema-guided LLM calls to parse unstructured text into structured JSON. A Validator Agent then checks completeness and accuracy before final output. Tracing spans handoffs, but lacks runtime validation.

Agents communicate via the SDK's handoff protocol, with tools for document loading and schema enforcement. Common patterns include pydantic models for output schemas and function calling for structured extraction. However, without output guardrails, agents consume unvalidated tool outputs, leading to propagation errors.

Vex integrates as a post-agent guardrail, validating every agent output against extraction schemas before handoff or final response.

risks.name: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
risks.description: Router misclassifies documents, causing ping-pong between Invoice and Contract extractors
risks.mitigation: Vex validates handoff instructions against document type schemas before execution

risks.name: Guardrail bypasses via prompt injection
risks.description: Malicious documents inject instructions that bypass schema enforcement in extractors
risks.mitigation: Vex output guardrails reject non-schema-compliant responses regardless of prompt content

risks.name: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
risks.description: Document loader returns malformed text that extractors parse incorrectly
risks.mitigation: Vex preprocesses tool outputs for format and content validity before agent consumption

risks.name: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
risks.description: Partial failures in validation agent aren't traced back to originating extractor
risks.mitigation: Vex provides end-to-end tracing with failure attribution across all handoffs

implementation.title: Schema-Guided Invoice Extractor Agent
implementation.description: Define Pydantic model and agent that extracts invoice data using function calling
implementation.code:
from agents import Agent, function_tool
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional

class InvoiceData(BaseModel):
    invoice_number: str = Field(..., description="Unique invoice ID")
    date: str = Field(..., description="Invoice date YYYY-MM-DD")
    total: float = Field(..., description="Total amount")
    vendor: str = Field(..., description="Vendor name")

@function_tool
def extract_invoice(text: str) -> InvoiceData:
    """Extract structured invoice data from text"""
    # LLM function calling implementation
    pass

invoice_agent = Agent(
    name="Invoice Extractor",
    instructions="Extract invoice data using the schema. Be precise.",
    tools=[extract_invoice]
)

implementation.title: Router Agent for Document Classification
implementation.description: Classifies documents and hands off to appropriate extractor

implementation.title: Validator Agent with Pydantic Checks
implementation.description: Validates completeness and type consistency of extracted data
implementation.code:
from agents import Agent
from pydantic import ValidationError

validator_agent = Agent(
    name="Data Validator",
    instructions="Validate extracted data for completeness and accuracy"
)

def validate_extraction(data: dict, schema: type) -> bool:
    try:
        validated = schema(**data)
        return all([valid
        getattr(validated, field) for field in schema.__fields__])
    except ValidationError:
        return False

# Called after each extraction
is_valid = validate_extraction(extracted_data, InvoiceData)

implementation.title: Multi-Agent Extraction Runner
implementation.description: Orchestrates full extraction pipeline with handoffs
implementation.code:
from agents import Runner

async def extract_from_document(document_text: str):
    # Route and extract
    classification = await router_agent.run(document_text)
    extracted_data = await Runner([router_agent, invoice_agent]).run(document_text)
    
    # Validate
    is_valid = validate_extraction(extracted_data, InvoiceData)
    
    if is_valid:
        return extracted_data
    raise ValueError("Extraction failed validation")

vexIntegration.title: Vex Output Guardrail on Extractor Agents
vexIntegration.description: Wrap agent outputs with Vex schema validation before handoff
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Guardrail, SchemaValidator
from agents import Agent

# Define Vex guardrail for invoice extraction
invoice_guardrail = Guardrail(
    name="invoice_schema_guardrail",
    validators=[
        SchemaValidator(
            pydantic_model=InvoiceData,
            checks=["schema_compliance", "field_accuracy", "completeness"]
        )
    ]
)

# Apply to agent
@vex.guardrail(invoice_guardrail)
def safe_invoice_extraction(text: str):
    result = invoice_agent.run(text)
    return result

# Usage
validated_invoice = safe_invoice_extraction(document_text)

vexIntegration.title: Handoff Validation Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Prevent loops by validating handoff instructions

vexIntegration.title: End-to-End Pipeline Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Comprehensive validation wrapping entire extraction flow
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import PipelineGuardrail

extraction_pipeline = PipelineGuardrail(
    name="data_extraction_pipeline",
    stages=[
        "document_loader",
        "router_classification",
        "schema_extraction",
        "completeness_validation"
    ],
    final_validator=SchemaValidator(InvoiceData)
)

@extraction_pipeline.guard
async def secure_extraction_pipeline(text: str):
    # Full agent workflow
    classification = await router_agent.run(text)
    data = await invoice_agent.run(text)
    return data

# Single call with full protection
result = await secure_extraction_pipeline(document)
